reason: The only residual finding is a minor index title mismatch (s3 titled "クラス図" instead of parent h2 "全体構造"). User impact is low because: (1) the hints for s3 already include both "全体構造" and "クラス図", so queries using either term will correctly retrieve the section; (2) the section content itself is accurate — it contains the class diagram image with correct description; (3) "クラス図" is arguably a more precise description of the actual section content (a single class diagram image), even if it reflects the inner h3 rather than the outer h2. No retrieval failure or content error results from this title choice.

findings_assessment.justification: The title 'クラス図' vs '全体構造' is a cosmetic naming discrepancy. The hints array for s3 already includes '全体構造', '全体構造図', and 'クラス図', so any user query containing either term will still route to this section. The section content (the class diagram image) is fully correct. No retrieval gap exists. Why it persisted through D→E: the Phase E agent output is byte-for-byte identical to Phase D — the fix was silently skipped, likely because the agent assessed the hints as sufficient compensation and the severity as too minor to warrant a structural change.
